questions.questionText:
What equal length of an iron wire and a copper-nickel alloy wire, each of 2 mm diameter connected parallel to give an equivalent resistance of 3$$\Omega$$ ?

(Given resistivities of iron and copper-nickel alloy wire are 12 $$\mu$$$$\Omega$$ and 51 $$\mu$$$$\Omega$$ cm respectively)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Out of all the patients in a hospital 89% are found to be suffering from heart ailment and 98% are suffering from lungs infection. If K% of them are suffering from both ailments, then K can not belong to the set :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The equation $$\arg \left( {{{z - 1} \over {z + 1}}} \right) = {\pi  \over 4}$$ represents a circle with :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the truth value of the Boolean expression $$\left( {\left( {p \vee q} \right) \wedge \left( {q \to r} \right) \wedge \left( { \sim r} \right)} \right) \to \left( {p \wedge q} \right)$$ is false, then the truth values of the statements p, q, r respectively can be :
questions.subject: Mathematics
Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $${{}^{20}{C_r}}$$ is the co-efficient of x<sup>r</sup> in the expansion of (1 + x)<sup>20</sup>, then the value of $$\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{20} {{r^2}.{}^{20}{C_r}} $$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let ABC be a triangle with A($$-$$3, 1) and $$\angle$$ACB = $$\theta$$, 0 < $$\theta$$ < $${\pi  \over 2}$$. If the equation of the median through B is 2x + y $$-$$ 3 = 0 and the equation of angle bisector of C is 7x $$-$$ 4y $$-$$ 1 = 0, then tan$$\theta$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$f(x) = \cos \left( {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sin \left( {{{\cot }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {{{1 - x} \over x}} } \right)} \right)$$, 0 < x < 1. Then :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow a  = \widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k$$ and $$\overrightarrow b  = \widehat j - \widehat k$$. If $$\overrightarrow c $$ is a vector such that $$\overrightarrow a  \times \overrightarrow c  = \overrightarrow b $$ and $$\overrightarrow a .\overrightarrow c  = 3$$, then $$\overrightarrow a .(\overrightarrow b  \times \overrightarrow c )$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $$\theta \in \left( {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right)$$. If the system of linear equations

$$(1 + {\cos ^2}\theta )x + {\sin ^2}\theta y + 4\sin 3\,\theta z = 0$$

$${\cos ^2}\theta x + (1 + {\sin ^2}\theta )y + 4\sin 3\,\theta z = 0$$

$${\cos ^2}\theta x + {\sin ^2}\theta y + (1 + 4\sin 3\,\theta )z = 0$$

has a non-trivial solution, then the value of $$\theta$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If a line along a chord of the circle 4x<sup>2</sup> + 4y<sup>2</sup> + 120x + 675 = 0, passes through the point ($$-$$30, 0) and is tangent to the parabola y<sup>2</sup> = 30x, then the length of this chord is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The sum of solutions of the equation

$${{\cos x} \over {1 + \sin x}} = \left| {\tan 2x} \right|$$, $$x \in \left( { - {\pi \over 2},{\pi \over 2}} \right) - \left\{ {{\pi \over 4}, - {\pi \over 4}} \right\}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let A and B be independent events such that P(A) = p, P(B) = 2p. The largest value of p, for which P (exactly one of A, B occurs) = $${5 \over 9}$$, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The value of

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } {1 \over n}\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{2n - 1} {{{{n^2}} \over {{n^2} + 4{r^2}}}} $$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A plane P contains the line  $$x + 2y + 3z + 1 = 0 = x - y - z - 6$$, and is perpendicular to the plane $$ - 2x + y + z + 8 = 0$$. Then which of the following points lies on P?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the sum of an infinite GP a, ar, ar<sup>2</sup>, ar<sup>3</sup>, ....... is 15 and the sum of the squares of its each term is 150, then the sum of ar<sup>2</sup>, ar<sup>4</sup>, ar<sup>6</sup>, ....... is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The mean and standard deviation of 20 observations were calculated as 10 and 2.5 respectively. It was found that by mistake one data value was taken as 25 instead of 35. if $$\alpha$$ and $$\sqrt \beta  $$ are the mean and standard deviation respectively for correct data, then ($$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$) is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let y = y(x) be a solution curve of the differential equation $$(y + 1){\tan ^2}x\,dx + \tan x\,dy + y\,dx = 0$$, $$x \in \left( {0,{\pi  \over 2}} \right)$$. If $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0 + } xy(x) = 1$$, then the value of $$y\left( {{\pi  \over 4}} \right)$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the line L be the projection of the line $${{x - 1} \over 2} = {{y - 3} \over 1} = {{z - 4} \over 2}$$ in the plane x $$-$$ 2y $$-$$ z = 3. If d is the distance of the point (0, 0, 6) from L, then d<sup>2</sup> is equal to _______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The area of the region $$S = \{ (x,y):3{x^2} \le 4y \le 6x + 24\} $$ is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: On the ellipse $${{{x^2}} \over 8} + {{{y^2}} \over 4} = 1$$ let P be a point in the second quadrant such that the tangent at P to the ellipse is perpendicular to the line x + 2y = 0. Let S and S' be the foci of the ellipse and e be its eccentricity. If A is the area of the triangle SPS' then, the value of (5 $$-$$ e<sup>2</sup>). A is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The value of $$\int\limits_{{{ - 1} \over {\sqrt 2 }}}^{{1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} {{{\left( {{{\left( {{{x + 1} \over {x - 1}}} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {{{x - 1} \over {x + 1}}} \right)}^2} - 2} \right)}^{{1 \over 2}}}dx} $$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A wire of length 36 m is cut into two pieces, one of the pieces is bent to form a square and the other is bent to form a circle. If the sum of the areas of the two figures is minimum, and the circumference of the circle is k (meter), then $$\left( {{4 \over \pi } + 1} \right)k$$ is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The sum of all integral values of k (k $$\ne$$ 0) for which the equation $${2 \over {x - 1}} - {1 \over {x - 2}} = {2 \over k}$$ in x has no real roots, is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The locus of a point, which moves such that the sum of squares of its distances from the points (0, 0), (1, 0), (0, 1), (1, 1) is 18 units, is a circle of diameter d. Then d<sup>2</sup> is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of three-digit even numbers, formed by the digits 0, 1, 3, 4, 6, 7 if the repetition of digits is not allowed, is ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A solid metal sphere of radius R having charge q is enclosed inside the concentric spherical shell of inner radius a and outer radius b as shown in the figure. The approximate variation electric field $$\overrightarrow E $$ as a function of distance r from centre O is given by
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An electric appliance supplies 6000 J/min heat to the system. If the system delivers a power of 90W. How long it would take to increase the internal energy by 2.5 $$\times$$ 10<sup>3</sup> J ?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particular hydrogen like ion emits radiation of frequency 2.92 $$\times$$ 10<sup>15</sup> Hz when it makes transition from n = 3 to n = 1. The frequency in Hz of radiation emitted in transition from n = 2 to n = 1 will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A series LCR circuit driven by 300 V at a frequency of 50 Hz contains a resistance R = 3 k$$\Omega$$, an inductor of inductive reactance X<sub>L</sub> = 250 $$\pi$$$$\Omega$$ and an unknown capacitor. The value of capacitance to maximize the average power should be : (Take $$\pi$$<sup>2</sup> = 10)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The initial mass of a rocket is 1000 kg. Calculate at what rate the fuel should be burnt so that the rocket is given an acceleration of 20 ms<sup>-2</sup>. The gases come out at a relative speed of 500 ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup> with respect to the rocket : [Use g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>]
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a Screw Gauge, fifth division of the circular scale coincides with the reference line when the ratchet is closed. There are 50 divisions on the circular scale, and the main scale moves by 0.5 mm on a complete rotation. For a particular observation the reading on the main scale is 5 mm and the 20<sup>th</sup> division of the circular scale coincides with reference line. Calculate the true reading.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If y = y(x) is an implicit function of x such that log<sub>e</sub>(x + y) = 4xy, then $${{{d^2}y} \over {d{x^2}}}$$ at x = 0 is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: An inductor coil stores 64 J of magnetic field energy and dissipates energy at the rate of 640 W when a current of 8A is passed through it. If this coil is joined across an ideal battery, find the time constant of the circuit in seconds :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If E, L, M and G denote the quantities as energy, angular momentum, mass and constant of gravitation respectively, then the dimensions of P in the formula P = EL<sup>2</sup>M<sup>$$-$$5</sup>G<sup>$$-$$2</sup> are :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Car B overtakes another car A at a relative speed of 40 ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. How fast will the image of car B appear to move in the mirror of focal length 10 cm fitted in car A, when the car B is 1.9 m away from the car A?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two narrow bores of diameter 5.0 mm and 8.0 mm are joined together to form a U-shaped tube open at both ends. If this U-tube contains water, what is the difference in the level of two limbs of the tube. [Take surface tension of water T = 7.3 $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$2</sup> Nm<sup>$$-$$1</sup>, angle of contact = 0, g = 10 ms<sup>2</sup> and density of water = 1.0 $$\times$$ 10<sup>3</sup> kg m<sup>$$-$$3</sup>]
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Identify the logic operation carried out by the given circuit :-
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a photoelectric experiment ultraviolet light of wavelength 280 nm is used with lithium cathode having work function $$\phi$$ = 2.5 eV. If the wavelength of incident light is switched to 400 nm, find out the change in the stopping potential. (h = 6.63 $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$34</sup> Js, c = 3 $$\times$$ 10<sup>8</sup> ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup>)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The material filled between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor has resistivity 200 $$\Omega$$m. The value of capacitance of the capacitor is 2 pF. If a potential difference of 40 V is applied across the plates of the capacitor, then the value of leakage current flowing out of the capacitor is : (given the value of relative permittivity of material is 50)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the given figure, the emf of the cell is 2.2 V and if internal resistance is 0.6$$\Omega$$. Calculate the power dissipated in the whole circuit :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Statement I : By doping silicon semiconductor with pentavalent material, the electrons density increases.

Statement II : The n-type semiconductor has net negative charge.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Statement - I is true but Statement - II is false.
(B) Statement - I is false but Statement - II is true.
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Inside a uniform spherical shell :

(1) the gravitational field is zero

(2) the gravitational potential is zero

(3) the gravitational field is same everywhere

(4) the gravitational potential is same everywhere

(5) all of the above

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The fractional change in the magnetic field intensity at a distance 'r' from centre on the axis of current carrying coil of radius 'a' to the magnetic field intensity at the centre of the same coil is : (Take r < a)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The rms speeds of the molecules of Hydrogen, Oxygen and Carbon dioxide at the same temperature are V<sub>H</sub>, V<sub>O</sub> and V<sub>C</sub> respectively then :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The magnitude of vectors $$\overrightarrow {OA} $$, $$\overrightarrow {OB} $$ and $$\overrightarrow {OC} $$ in the given figure are equal. The direction of $$\overrightarrow {OA} $$ + $$\overrightarrow {OB} $$ $$-$$ $$\overrightarrow {OC} $$ with x-axis will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A soap bubble of radius 3 cm is formed inside the another soap bubble of radius 6 cm. The radius of an equivalent soap bubble which has the same excess pressure as inside the smaller bubble with respect to the atmospheric pressure is ................ cm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two short magnetic dipoles m1 and m2 each having magnetic moment of 1 Am2 are placed at point O and P respectively. The distance between OP is 1 meter. The torque experienced by the magnetic dipole m2 due to the presence of m1 is ........... $$\times$$ 10$$-$$7 Nm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The electric field in a plane electromagnetic wave is given by

$$\overrightarrow E = 200\cos \left[ {\left( {{{0.5 \times {{10}^3}} \over m}} \right)x - \left( {1.5 \times {{10}^{11}}{{rad} \over s} \times t} \right)} \right]{V \over m}\widehat j$$. If this wave falls normally on a perfectly reflecting surface having an area of 100 cm2. If the radiation pressure exerted by the E.M. wave on the surface during a 10 minute exposure is $${x \over {{{10}^9}}}{N \over {{m^2}}}$$. Find the value of x .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Two travelling waves produces a standing wave represented by equation,

y = 1.0 mm cos(1.57 cm$$-$$1) x sin(78.5 s$$-$$1)t.

The node closest to the origin in the region x > 0 will be at x = .............. cm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two spherical balls having equal masses with radius of 5 cm each are thrown upwards along the same vertical direction at an interval of 3s with the same initial velocity of 35 m/s, then these balls collide at a height of ............... m. (Take g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A source and a detector move away from each other in absence of wind with a speed of 20 m/s with respect to the ground. If the detector detects a frequency of 1800 Hz of the sound coming from the source, then the original frequency of source considering speed of sound in air 340 m/s will be ............... Hz.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: White light is passed through a double slit and interference is observed on a screen 1.5 m away. The separation between the slits is 0.3 mm. The first violet and red fringes are formed 2.0 mm and 3.5 mm away from the central white fringes. the difference in wavelengths of red and violet light is ................ nm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
An amplitude modulated wave is represented by
Cm(t) = 10(1 + 0.2 cos 12560t) sin(111 $$\times$$ 104t) volts. The modulating frequency in kHz will be ................. .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Excess of isobutane on reaction with Br<sub>2</sub> in presence of light at 125$$^\circ$$C gives which one of the following, as the major product?
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A uniform chain of length 3 meter and mass 3 kg overhangs a smooth table with 2 meter lying on the table. If k is the kinetic energy of the chain in joule as it completely slips off the table, then the value of k is ................. . (Take g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The conversion of hydroxyapatite occurs due to presence of F<sup>$$-$$</sup> ions in water. The correct formula of hydroxyapatite is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Consider a badminton racket with length scales as shown in the figure.


If the mass of the linear and circular portions of the badminton racket are same (M) and the mass of the threads are negligible, the moment of inertia of the racket about an axis perpendicular to the handle and in the plane of the ring at, $${r \over 2}$$ distance from the end A of the handle will be ................ Mr2.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: What are the products formed in sequence when excess of CO<sub>2</sub> is passed in slaked lime?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The incorrect statement is :
options: (A) Cl<sub>2</sub> is more reactive than ClF.
(B) F<sub>2</sub> is more reactive than ClF.
(C) On hydrolysis ClF forms HOCl and HF.
(D) F<sub>2</sub> is a stronger oxidizing agent than Cl<sub>2</sub> in aqueous solution.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The major product formed in the following reaction is :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The limiting molar conductivity of KCl (strong electrolyte) is higher compared to that of CH3COOH (weak electrolyte).

Statement II : Molar conductivity decreases with decrease in concentration of electrolyte.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
(B) Statement I is false but Statement II is true.
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I : According to Bohr's model of an atom, qualitatively the magnitude of velocity of electron increases with decrease in positive charges on the nucleus as there is no strong hold on the electron by the nucleus.

Statement II : According to Bohr's model of an atom, qualitatively the magnitude of velocity of electron increases with decrease in principal quantum number.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(C) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(D) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following methods is most suitable for preparing deionized water?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The polymer formed on heating Novolac with formaldehyde is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.

Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below :
options: (A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The major product formed in the following reaction is :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Among the following compounds I-IV, which one forms a yellow precipitate on reacting sequentially with (i) NaOH (ii) dil. HNO3 (iii) AgNO3?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The major products formed in the following reaction sequence A and B are :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following complexes is violet in colour?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The choice of reducing agents for metals extraction can be made by using Ellingham diagram, a plot of $$\Delta$$G vs temperature.

Statement II : The value of $$\Delta$$S increases from left to right in Ellingham diagram.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(B) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(D) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The correct options for the products A and B of the following reactions are :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I : In the titration between strong acid and weak base methyl orange is suitable as an indicator.

Statement II : For titration of acetic acid with NaOH phenolphthalein is not a suitable indicator.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(B) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct for the adsorption of a gas at a given temperature on a solid surface?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following when dissolved in water gives coloured solution in nitrogen atmosphere?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The Born-Haber cycle for KCl is evaluated with the following data :

$${\Delta _f}{H^\Theta }$$ for KCl = $$-$$436.7 kJ mol$$-$$1 ;

$${\Delta _{sub}}{H^\Theta }$$ for K = 89.2 kJ mol$$-$$1 ;

$${\Delta _{ionization}}{H^\Theta }$$ for K = 419.0 kJ mol$$-$$1 ;

$${\Delta _{electron\,gain}}{H^\Theta }$$ for Cl(g) = $$-$$348.6 kJ mol$$-$$1 ;

$${\Delta _{bond}}{H^\Theta }$$ for Cl2 = 243.0 kJ mol$$-$$1

The magnitude of lattice enthalpy of KCl in kJ mol$$-$$1 is _____________ (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An aqueous KCl solution of density 1.20 g mL<sup>$$-$$1</sup> has a molality of 3.30 mol kg<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. The molarity of the solution in mol L<sup>$$-$$1</sup> is ____________ (Nearest integer) [Molar mass of KCl = 74.5]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The correct sequential addition of reagents in the preparation of 3-nitrobenzoic acid from benzene is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The following data was obtained for chemical reaction given below at 975 K.

2NO(g) + 2H2(g) $$\to$$ N2(g) + 2H2O(g)



The order of the reaction with respect to NO is ___________. [Integer answer]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The ratio of number of water molecules in Mohr's salt and potash alum is ____________ $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The total number of negative charge in the tetrapeptide, Gly-Glu-Asp-Tyr, at pH 12.5 will be ______________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The number of 4f electrons in the ground statement electronic configuration of Gd<sup>2+</sup> is ___________. [Atomic number of Gd = 64]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Of the following four aqueous solutions, total number of those solutions whose freezing point is lower than that of 0.10 M C2H5OH is __________ (Integer answer)

(i) 0.10 M Ba3(PO4)2

(ii) 0.10 M Na2SO4

(iii) 0.10 M KCl

(iv) 0.10 M Li3PO4
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The OH$$-$$ concentration in a mixture of 5.0 mL of 0.0504 M NH4Cl and 2 mL of 0.0210 M NH3 solution is x $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 M. The value of x is ___________. (Nearest integer)

[Given Kw = 1 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$14 and Kb = 1.8 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: AB<sub>3</sub> is an interhalogen T-shaped molecule. The number of lone pairs of electrons on A is __________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
These are physical properties of an element

(A) Sublimation enthalpy

(B) Ionisation enthalpy

(C) Hydration enthalpy

(D) Electron gain enthalpy

The total number of above properties that affect the reduction potential is ____________ (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry
